sections.instruction: Questions 1-3: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER. Questions 4-10: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.
sections.passage_or_context:
TOTAL INSURANCE INCIDENT REPORT
Name: Michael Alexander
Address: 24 Mainly Street, {{BLANK}}, Sydney
Shipping agent: {{BLANK}}
Place of origin: China
Date of arrival: {{BLANK}}
Reference number: 601ACK

sections.instruction: Question 11: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 12-14: Label the plan below. Write NO MORE THAN WORDS. Questions 15-20: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: According to the speaker, the main purposes of the park are
options: A education and entertainment
B research and education
C research and entertainment

questions.content: When are the experimental areas closed to the public?
options: A all the year round
B almost all the year
C a short time every year

questions.content: How can you move around the park?
options: A by tram, walking or bicycle
B by solar car or bicycle
C by bicycle, walking or bus

questions.content: The rare breed animals kept in the park include
options: A hens and horses
B goats and cows
C goats and hens

questions.content: What is the main purpose of having the Rare Breeds Section?
options: A to save unusual animals
B to keep a variety of breeds
C to educate the public

questions.content: What can you see in the park at the present time?
options: A the arrival of wild birds
B fruit tree blossom
C a demonstration of fishing

questions.content: The shop contains books about
options: A animals
B local traditions
C the history of the park

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 25-30: Complete the summary below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.
sections.passage_or_context:
Honey Bees in Australia

LOOKING FOR ASIAN HONEY BEES
Birds called Rainbow Bee Eaters eat only {{BLANK}} and cough up small bits of skeleton and other products in a pellet.
Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for {{BLANK}}
They collect the pellets and take them to a {{BLANK}} for analysis.
Here {{BLANK}} is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the {{BLANK}} of Asian bees in the pellets.
The benefit of this research is that the result is more {{BLANK}} than searching for live Asian bees.

questions.content: Where in Australia have Asian honey bees been found in the past?
options: A Queensland
B New South Wales
C several states

questions.content: A problem with Asian honey bees is that they
options: A attack native bees
B carry parasites
C damage crops

questions.content: What point is made about Australian bees?
options: A Their honey varies in quality
B Their size stops them from pollinating some flowers
C They are sold to customers abroad

questions.content: Grant Freeman says that if Asian honey bees got into Australia,
options: A the country’s economy would be affected
B they could be used in the study of allergies
C certain areas of agriculture would benefit

questions.content: Birds called Rainbow Bee Eaters eat only {{BLANK}} and cough up small bits of skeleton and other products in a pellet. Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for {{BLANK}}. They collect the pellets and take them to a {{BLANK}} for analysis. Here {{BLANK}} is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the {{BLANK}} of Asian bees in the pellets. The benefit of this research is that the result is more {{BLANK}} than searching for live Asian bees.

sections.instruction: Questions 31-36: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 37-40: Which statement applies to each of the following people who were interviewed by Shona? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to questions 37-40.
sections.passage_or_context:
Research on questions about doctors

People interviewed by Shona
37. a person interviewed in the street
38. an undergraduate at the university
39. a colleague in her department
40. a tutor in a foreign university

questions.content: In order to set up her research programme, Shona got
options: A advice from personal friends in other countries
B help from students in- other countries
C information from her tutor’s contacts in other countries

questions.content: What types of people were included in the research?
options: A young people in their first job
B men who were working
C women who were unemployed

questions.content: Shona says that in her questionnaire her aim was
options: A to get a wide range of data
B to limit people’s responses
C to guide people through interviews.

questions.content: What do Shona’s initial results show about medical services in Britain?
options: A Current concerns are misrepresented by the press
B Financial issues are critical to the government
C Reforms within hospitals have been unsuccessful

questions.content: Shona needs to do further research in order to
options: A present the government with her findings
B decide the level of extra funding needed
C identify the preferences of the public

questions.content: Shona has learnt from the research project that
options: A it is important to plan projects carefully
B people do not like answering questions
C colleagues do not always agree

questions.content: Which statement applies to each of the following people who were interviewed by Shona?
options: 37. a person interviewed in the street
38. an undergraduate at the university
39. a colleague in her department
40. a tutor in a foreign university
